text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other's code, they can catch bugs early before the code reaches production. This saves time and money because fixing issues later is much more difficult. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while senior developers gain fresh perspectives. Over time, this process naturally improves overall team quality, as everyone becomes familiar with the codebase and best practices. In short, code reviews are not just about finding mistakes—they are about building a stronger, more collaborative team.
